content.problemStatement: Write a C program that swaps the values of two integer variables, a =5 and b = 10, and prints their new values using a single printf statement with a space between them
content.outputFormat: Two space-separated integers on a single line representing the swapped values of a and b.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The program swaps a=5 and b=10 and prints the swapped values.
No input is required; the output is always the same swapped pair.
Repeated to emphasize that the result does not depend on any external data.

solution.approach: The program initializes two integers a = 5 and b = 10. It then swaps their values using a temporary variable (or tuple assignment) and prints the swapped values. Since there is no input, the same output is produced for every execution.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
